abstract: Federated Averaging trains local client models and averages their weight updates on a server.
FedProx adds a proximal term to stabilize federated learning under non-IID client data.
Secure aggregation hides individual client updates while allowing the server to recover the aggregate.
Differential privacy in federated learning clips and noises updates to reduce gradient leakage.
Client drift happens when local training moves models in different directions because client data distributions differ.
Edge AI runs inference near sensors to reduce latency and dependence on cloud connectivity.
TinyML deploys compact neural networks on microcontrollers with strict memory and energy limits.
Model compression reduces model size using pruning, quantization, distillation, or low-rank methods.
Edge inference can improve privacy because raw sensor data may stay on the local device.
Embedded AI must respect memory, compute, battery, and thermal constraints during deployment.
Int8 quantization stores weights or activations with 8-bit integers to reduce memory and bandwidth.
Low-bit quantization uses very few bits per parameter and may damage accuracy if too aggressive.
Weight pruning removes small or low-importance weights to create sparse neural networks.
Structured pruning removes channels, filters, or blocks so hardware can accelerate the sparse model.
Knowledge distillation trains a smaller student model to match a larger teacher model.
Convolutional networks learn local filters that detect edges, textures, parts, and objects.
Vision Transformers split images into patches and process them as token sequences with self-attention.
Swin Transformer uses shifted local windows and hierarchy for efficient visual attention.
Image augmentation applies transformations such as crop, flip, color jitter, and rotation during training.
Semantic segmentation assigns a class label to every pixel in an image.
Object detection predicts object classes and bounding boxes in an image.
YOLO performs object detection in a single forward pass and is often used for real-time detection.
Faster R-CNN uses region proposals before classification and bounding-box refinement.
Anchor boxes are predefined box shapes used by some detectors to predict object locations.
Mean average precision summarizes object detection precision and recall across classes and IoU thresholds.
Retrieval augmented generation retrieves evidence documents before generating an answer.
Dense retrieval maps queries and documents into embedding vectors and ranks by vector similarity.
BM25 is a sparse lexical retrieval method based on term frequency, inverse document frequency, and document length.
Reranking applies a stronger model to reorder initially retrieved documents for relevance.
Hit at k measures whether the correct document appears within the top k retrieved results.
Language model hallucination occurs when a model produces fluent but unsupported or false claims.
Prompt engineering studies how instructions, examples, and constraints change model behavior.
Calibration measures whether predicted probabilities match observed correctness frequencies.
Toxicity evaluation measures whether model outputs contain harmful, abusive, or unsafe language.
LLM-as-judge evaluation uses a language model to rate outputs according to a rubric.
Tool-using agents call calculators, search systems, or code tools to solve tasks more reliably.
Planning agents decompose a goal into steps before executing actions.
Reflection loops let an agent critique its own output and revise the next action.
Agent memory stores useful prior context so later decisions can use earlier information.
Tool routing selects the correct external tool based on task intent and input constraints.
Degree centrality measures how many direct connections a node has in a graph.
Betweenness centrality measures how often a node lies on shortest paths between other nodes.
Community detection finds groups of nodes with dense internal connections.
Graph convolution aggregates neighboring node features to learn graph-aware representations.
Node classification predicts labels for graph nodes using features and graph structure.
Cross-validation trains and tests models across multiple data splits to estimate generalization.
A confusion matrix shows correct and incorrect predictions for each class.
Macro F1 averages class F1 scores equally and is useful when class balance matters.
ROC-AUC measures how well a classifier ranks positive examples above negative examples.
Early stopping halts training when validation performance no longer improves.
Selection rate compares how often a model predicts a positive outcome for different groups.
Equal opportunity compares true positive rates across demographic groups.
Privacy attacks can infer sensitive information from model outputs or gradients.
A model card documents intended use, limitations, training data, and evaluation results.
A datasheet for datasets documents data collection, composition, recommended use, and risks.
Dropout randomly disables neurons during training to reduce overfitting.
Weight decay penalizes large model weights to improve generalization.
Batch normalization stabilizes activations and can speed up neural network training.
Learning rate schedules change the optimizer step size during training.
Adam combines momentum and adaptive learning rates for gradient-based optimization.
MNIST contains handwritten digit images and is often used for basic classification experiments.
FashionMNIST contains grayscale clothing images across ten fashion categories.
CIFAR-10 contains small color images from ten object classes.
COCO provides images with object detection, segmentation, and caption annotations.
Cora is a citation network dataset with paper features, citation links, and topic labels.
ONNX export converts trained models into an interoperable format for deployment.
Latency benchmarking measures how long inference takes under deployment conditions.
Throughput measures how many inputs a system processes per unit time.
Data drift monitoring checks whether production inputs change compared with training data.
Rollback strategy restores a previous model when a new deployment causes problems.
Forecasting predicts future values from historical time-series observations.
Seasonality is a repeated temporal pattern such as daily, weekly, or yearly cycles.
Anomaly detection finds unusual observations that differ from expected behavior.
Rolling-window evaluation trains on past windows and tests on later time periods.
Temporal leakage happens when future information leaks into time-series training features.
Tokenization splits text into units such as words, subwords, or characters.
Named entity recognition finds entities such as people, organizations, and locations in text.
Text classification assigns labels such as topic, intent, or sentiment to documents.
Summarization produces a shorter version of a document while preserving important information.
Machine translation converts text from one language into another language.
Adversarial examples are inputs modified to fool a model while appearing similar to humans.
Data poisoning attacks inject harmful training examples to corrupt model behavior.
Model extraction tries to copy a model by querying it and observing outputs.
Prompt injection tries to override an LLM application's instructions using malicious input.
Membership inference predicts whether a sample was part of a model's training data.
Gradient descent updates parameters in the direction that reduces loss.
Momentum accumulates past gradients to smooth and accelerate optimization.
Learning rate controls the step size of parameter updates during training.
Loss landscape describes how the objective changes across parameter space.
Hyperparameter search tests settings such as learning rate, depth, and regularization strength.
Q-learning estimates action values and learns a policy from rewards without requiring a model of the environment.
Policy gradient methods directly optimize a parameterized policy using reward-weighted gradient estimates.
A reward function defines the feedback signal that guides a reinforcement learning agent toward desired behavior.
Exploration tries new actions while exploitation uses actions that currently look best.
Offline reinforcement learning trains policies from fixed logged data without new environment interaction.
Vision-language models connect images and text so a system can reason across both modalities.
Image captioning generates natural-language descriptions for visual content.
CLIP-style contrastive learning aligns image and text embeddings using paired examples.
Multimodal embeddings place different data types in a shared representation space for retrieval or reasoning.
Visual question answering answers text questions about the content of an image.
